justification: Meets all pass criteria: recommends 5 services with strong justification tied to team size (explicitly states '5 services is the upper bound' for 8 engineers); Location Tracking is handled within Courier Service with CQRS for 5K writes/sec; Payment is correctly separated for consistency/PCI reasons; team size constraint is central to the design; Strangler Fig is the recommended migration pattern with a detailed extraction sequence; trade_offs explicitly cover operational overhead ('5 deployment pipelines, 5 sets of health checks, distributed tracing needed') and distributed tracing complexity; risks include data consistency/distributed transaction problems across services. The only minor note is 5 services rather than 4, but the justification is thorough and the rubric accepts 3-5.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 5 services recommended with thorough justification; Location Tracking identified for its 5K writes/sec requiring time-series DB; Payment correctly separated for ACID/PCI; team size constraint explicitly addressed (8 engineers across 5 services with pair ownership); Strangler Fig migration pattern recommended with phased extraction; trade_offs covers operational overhead and distributed tracing/debugging complexity; risks includes distributed transaction/saga failure handling and premature decomposition concerns. The decision to merge courier assignment + location tracking into one Courier Operations service demonstrates strong architectural judgment about avoiding unnecessary distributed consistency problems.

observations.text: Excellent justification for merging assignment and location tracking — correctly identifies that splitting would create a distributed consistency problem since the assignment algorithm needs real-time location data
Strangler Fig migration with a well-reasoned extraction order: Payment first (learn on lower-risk), Courier Ops second (scaling pressure), Notifications/Accounts third, Orders last
Team constraint explicitly addressed with concrete pair ownership model: 4 pairs across 5 services, with low-change services sharing a pair
Saga failure handling risk identified with specific mitigation (outbox pattern, reconciliation job for orphaned authorizations)
Trade-offs are genuine sacrifices, not just restated benefits — e.g., eventual consistency windows, choreography becoming tangled with more participants
CQRS scoped appropriately to location tracking only, with explicit warning against premature event sourcing system-wide
5 services is at the upper bound of the 3-5 range for 8 engineers — could have more strongly advocated for starting with 3-4 and deferring Customer Accounts extraction

trials.agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Decompose the courier platform monolith into 5 services aligned to bounded contexts: Orders, Courier Operations (assignment + location tracking), Payments, Notifications, and Customer Accounts. The split is driven by three forces: (1) deployment coupling — payments and location tracking have radically different change cadences and risk profiles, (2) scaling independence — location tracking at 5K writes/sec needs a fundamentally different data store and scaling strategy than

trials.justification: Meets all pass criteria. Recommends 6 services but explicitly acknowledges team size constraint ('this is the maximum safe decomposition — more services would exceed operational capacity') and proposes phased extraction of only 2 at a time. Location Tracking correctly identified as separate due to 5K writes/sec with dedicated time-series store and CQRS. Payment correctly separated for consistency and PCI-adjacent concerns. Strangler Fig migration explicitly recommended in 3 phases over 9-12 months. Trade-offs include operational overhead ('some services will share on-call rotation') and distributed tracing complexity (listed in risks as observability investment). Risks include distributed transaction/saga failure and premature decomposition. The 6-service count is at the edge of the rubric's 3-5 range, but MJ explicitly defends it as the maximum and notes Customer Service could merge with Order Service, showing awareness of the constraint.

observations.text: Location Tracking thoroughly analyzed: dedicated time-series store, CQRS pattern, batch writes, load test recommendation at 2x peak
Payment Service correctly isolated with idempotency keys, exactly-once semantics, and isolated database
Strangler Fig migration with concrete 3-phase plan and rollback criteria
Saga orchestration pattern for Order-Payment-Courier flow with explicit compensation logic
Risks section is comprehensive: operational maturity gap, data consistency during migration, saga failure, event ordering, premature extraction
Trade-offs section includes concrete alternatives considered for each decision
Recommends 6 services rather than 4-5, slightly exceeding the ideal range for 8 engineers. MJ acknowledges this tension but still lands on 6.
Low-confidence areas honestly note that Customer Service could merge with Order Service, showing self-awareness about the 6-service risk
Phased approach (2 services at a time) effectively mitigates the operational burden concern even with 6 total services
